title: 1.1: Tests that RandUUID default behavior (no arguments) generates version 4 UUIDs with command representation as 'RandUUID()'
1.2: Tests that RandUUID._fix() returns version 4 UUIDs when version=4 is explicitly passed and two generated UUIDs are different
1.3: Tests that RandUUID._fix() returns version 1 UUIDs preserving provided node and clock_seq values when various parameter combinations are provided
1.4: Tests that RandUUID._fix() returns version 1 UUIDs when only node or clock_seq parameters are provided without explicit version
1.5: Tests that RandUUID(version=5, namespace, name) generates version 5 UUIDs, outputs formatted command() string, produces identical bytes for same inputs
1.6: Tests that RandUUID(version=3, namespace, name) generates version 3 UUIDs, produces identical bytes for same inputs, and differs from implicit namespace+name (which generates version 5)
1.7: Tests that converting RandUUID to string produces a valid UUID format matching the pattern [0-9a-f]{8}(-[0-9a-f]{4}){3}-[0-9a-f]{12}
1.8: Tests that RandUUID with template preserves static hex segments, randomizes wildcard positions, and outputs formatted command() string
1.9: Tests that RandUUID with a template containing range values generates UUIDs within the specified hex range
2.1: Places the test for RandUUID default behaviour in test/regression.uts
2.2: Places the test for RandUUID version 4 UUID generation in test/regression.uts
2.3: Places the test for RandUUID version 1 UUID generation in test/regression.uts
2.4: Places the test for RandUUID version 1 implicit version detection in test/regression.uts
2.5: Places the test for RandUUID version 5 UUID generation in test/regression.uts
2.6: Places the test for RandUUID version 3 UUID generation in test/regression.uts
2.7: Places the test for RandUUID string format validation in test/regression.uts
2.8: Places the test for RandUUID template with static parts in test/regression.uts
2.9: Places the test for RandUUID template with range values in test/regression.uts
3.1: All tests use the UTScapy framework with test blocks starting with '= <test_name>'
3.2: All tests follow the naming pattern '= RandUUID <description>' for test block headers
3.3: All tests use Python assert statements for test assertions
